stemHtml: During a cold winter day, a jet aircraft is performing a short flight is in the cruise at a medium level. Both pilots note that the EGT is increasing, however the thrust levers have not moved. What could be the reason for this?

options.a: Because the air is colder, the temperature in the combustion chamber has to be increased to compensate.
options.b: The fuel density is higher due to the low temperature, so more fuel enters the combustion chamber.
options.c: Ice is forming on the leading edge of the wings, so more thrust is added to compensate.
options.d: Ice is forming around the engine intake, causing restricted airflow.
